questions.questionText: Let m and M respectively be the minimum and the maximum values of $$f(x) = {\sin ^{ - 1}}2x + \sin 2x + {\cos ^{ - 1}}2x + \cos 2x,\,x \in \left[ {0,{\pi \over 8}} \right]$$. Then m + M is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If xy4 attains maximum value at the point (x, y) on the line passing through the points (50 + $$\alpha$$, 0) and (0, 50 + $$\alpha$$), $$\alpha$$ > 0, then (x, y) also lies on the line :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$\alpha$$1, $$\alpha$$2 ($$\alpha$$1 < $$\alpha$$2) be the values of $$\alpha$$ fo the points ($$\alpha$$, $$-$$3), (2, 0) and (1, $$\alpha$$) to be collinear. Then the equation of the line, passing through ($$\alpha$$1, $$\alpha$$2) and making an angle of $${\pi \over 3}$$ with the positive direction of the x-axis, is :

questions.questionText: The real part of the complex number $${{{{(1 + 2i)}^8}\,.\,{{(1 - 2i)}^2}} \over {(3 + 2i)\,.\,\overline {(4 - 6i)} }}$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: For two positive real numbers a and b such that $${1 \over {{a^2}}} + {1 \over {{b^3}}} = 4$$, then minimum value of the constant term in the expansion of $${\left( {a{x^{{1 \over 8}}} + b{x^{ - {1 \over {12}}}}} \right)^{10}}$$ is :

questions.questionText: Let S be the set of all integral values of $$\alpha$$ for which the sum of squares of two real roots of the quadratic equation $$3{x^2} + (\alpha - 6)x + (\alpha + 3) = 0$$ is minimum. Then S :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$\alpha = \tan \left( {{{5\pi } \over {16}}\sin \left( {2{{\cos }^{ - 1}}\left( {{1 \over {\sqrt 5 }}} \right)} \right)} \right)$$ and $$\beta = \cos \left( {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left( {{4 \over 5}} \right) + {{\sec }^{ - 1}}\left( {{5 \over 3}} \right)} \right)$$ where the inverse trigonometric functions take principal values. Then, the equation whose roots are $$\alpha$$ and $$\beta$$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let the eccentricity of the ellipse $${x^2} + {a^2}{y^2} = 25{a^2}$$ be b times the eccentricity of the hyperbola $${x^2} - {a^2}{y^2} = 5$$, where a is the minimum distance between the curves y = ex and y = logex. Then $${a^2} + {1 \over {{b^2}}}$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$A = \left[ {\matrix{ 1 & { - 2} & \alpha \cr \alpha & 2 & { - 1} \cr } } \right]$$ and $$B = \left[ {\matrix{ 2 & \alpha \cr { - 1} & 2 \cr 4 & { - 5} \cr } } \right],\,\alpha \in C$$. Then the absolute value of the sum of all values of $$\alpha$$ for which det(AB) = 0 is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
The probability distribution of X is :

X 0 1 2 3
P(X) $${{1 - d} \over 4}$$ $${{1 + 2d} \over 4}$$ $${{1 - 4d} \over 4}$$ $${{1 + 3d} \over 4}$$

For the minimum possible value of d, sixty times the mean of X is equal to _______________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If for some $$\alpha$$ > 0, the area of the region $$\{ (x,y):|x + \alpha | \le y \le 2 - |x|\} $$ is equal to $${3 \over 2}$$, then the area of the region $$\{ (x,y):0 \le y \le x + 2\alpha ,\,|x| \le 1\} $$ is equal to ____________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Consider a triangle ABC whose vertices are A(0, $$\alpha$$, $$\alpha$$), B($$\alpha$$, 0, $$\alpha$$) and C($$\alpha$$, $$\alpha$$, 0), $$\alpha$$ > 0. Let D be a point moving on the line x + z $$-$$ 3 = 0 = y and G be the centroid of $$\Delta$$ABC. If the minimum length of GD is $$\sqrt {{{57} \over 2}} $$, then $$\alpha$$ is equal to ____________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$f(t) = \int\limits_0^t {{e^{{x^3}}}\left( {{{{x^8}} \over {{{({x^6} + 2{x^3} + 2)}^2}}}} \right)dx} $$. If $$f(1) + f'(1) = \alpha e - {1 \over 6}$$, then the value of 150$$\alpha$$ is equal to ___________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Suppose $$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0} {{F(x)} \over {{x^3}}}$$ exists and is equal to L, where

$$F(x) = \left| {\matrix{ {a + \sin {x \over 2}} & { - b\cos x} & 0 \cr { - b\cos x} & 0 & {a + \sin {x \over 2}} \cr 0 & {a + \sin {x \over 2}} & { - b\cos x} \cr } } \right|$$.

Then, $$-$$112 L is equal to ___________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: A hostel has 100 students. On a certain day (consider it day zero) it was found that two students are infected with some virus. Assume that the rate at which the virus spreads is directly proportional to the product of the number of infected students and the number of non-infected students. If the number of infected students on 4th day is 30, then number of infected students on 8th day will be __________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: An electric cable of copper has just one wire of radius 9 mm. Its resistance is 14 $$\Omega$$. If this single copper wire of the cable is replaced by seven identical well insulated copper wires each of radius 3 mm connected in parallel, then the new resistance of the combination will be :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:

If the reaction is of first order, the temperature at which the rate constant is 2 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$6 s$$-$$1 is _____________ $$\times$$ 10$$-$$1 K.

2Cu2O + Cu2S $$\to$$ 6Cu + SO2

questions.questionText: The distance of the point (3, 2, $$-$$1) from the plane $$3x - y + 4z + 1 = 0$$ along the line $${{2 - x} \over 2} = {{y - 3} \over 2} = {{z + 1} \over 1}$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If a random variable X follows the Binomial distribution B(5, p) such that P(X = 0) = P(X = 1), then $${{P(X = 2)} \over {P(X = 3)}}$$ is equal to :

questions.questionText: Let for $$f(x) = {a_0}{x^2} + {a_1}x + {a_2},\,f'(0) = 1$$ and $$f'(1) = 0$$. If a0, a1, a2 are in an arithmatico-geometric progression, whose corresponding A.P. has common difference 1 and corresponding G.P. has common ratio 2, then f(4) is equal to _____________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
An employee of a factory moving away from his workplace by a car listens to the siren of the factory. He drives the car at the speed of 72 kmh$$-$$1 in the direction of wind which is blowing at 72 kmh$$-$$1 speed. Frequency of siren is 720 Hz. The employee hears an apparent frequency of ____________ Hz.

(Assume speed of sound to be 340 ms$$-$$1)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
An element X has a body centred cubic (bcc) structure with a cell edge of 200 pm. The density of the element is 5 g cm$$-$$3. The number of atoms present in 300 g of the element X is _______________.

Given : Avogadro constant, NA = 6.0 $$\times$$ 1023 mol$$-$$1.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Let a vector $$\overrightarrow c $$ be coplanar with the vectors $$\overrightarrow a = - \widehat i + \widehat j + \widehat k$$ and $$\overrightarrow b = 2\widehat i + \widehat j - \widehat k$$. If the vector $$\overrightarrow c $$ also satisfies the conditions $$\overrightarrow c \,.\,\left[ {\left( {\overrightarrow a + \overrightarrow b } \right) \times \left( {\overrightarrow a \times \overrightarrow b } \right)} \right] = - 42$$ and $$\left( {\overrightarrow c \times \left( {\overrightarrow a - \overrightarrow b } \right)} \right)\,.\,\widehat k = 3$$, then the value of $$|\overrightarrow c {|^2}$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: A speech signal given by 11 sin(2200 $$\pi$$t) V is used for amplitude modulation with a carrier signal given by 44 sin(6600 $$\pi$$t) V. The minimum amplitude of modulated wave will be :

questions.questionText: The circuit diagram of potentiometer used to measure the internal resistance of a cell (E) is shown in figure. The key 'K' is kept closed so as to send constant current through potentiometer wire. When key 'K1' is kept open the null point is found to be at 120 cm on the potentiometer wire. When the key 'K1' is closed the null point is shifted at 80 cm at the potentiometer wire. The internal resistance of the given cell is _____________ $$\Omega$$.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Consider three circles:

$${C_1}:{x^2} + {y^2} = {r^2}$$

$${C_2}:{(x - 1)^2} + {(y - 1)^2} = {r^2}$$

$${C_3}:{(x - 2)^2} + {(y - 1)^2} = {r^2}$$

If a line L : y = mx + c be a common tangent to C1, C2 and C3 such that C1 and C3 lie on one side of line L while C2 lies on other side, then the value of $$20({r^2} + c)$$ is equal to :
